question_text: A ship uses sonar to detect an underwater object. The sound wave travels down through seawater at 1500 m/s, bounces off an object, and returns to the ship. The total time taken is 4 seconds. Calculate the distance from the ship to the object.
explanation: The sound travels down to the object and back up again, so the total distance travelled is speed × time = 1500 × 4 = 6000 m. Since this is the round trip, the distance to the object is 6000 ÷ 2 = 3000 m. Remember: echo problems always involve a return journey!
